name: Dura-Europos house church
description: Often cited as the earliest excavated house church complex, adapted from a domestic structure with a baptistery and Christian wall art.
uncertainty: The exact date-range of adaptation is debated within the mid-3rd century archaeological horizon.

name: Synagogue with murals/icons
description: The Dura-Europos synagogue preserves extensive figurative wall paintings, providing context for late antique visual culture alongside nearby Christian sites.
uncertainty: Iconographic interpretation and liturgical use are still debated in scholarship.

name: House church with fish mosaic and altar
description: Placeholder feature for a reported early worship space linked in some accounts to fish-mosaic Christian imagery and altar-like installations.
uncertainty: This record is intentionally provisional: chronology, interpretation, and site identification may be revised as evidence is evaluated.

name: Church of the Holy Sepulchre
description: Major Constantinian basilica complex associated with Jesus' crucifixion and resurrection traditions; a key pilgrimage center in late antiquity.
uncertainty: The archaeology of earlier phases and exact 4th-century liturgical circulation is still debated in detail.

name: Church of the Nativity (Bethlehem)
description: Imperial-era basilica at Bethlehem tied to the nativity pilgrimage tradition; repeatedly rebuilt and restored.
uncertainty: The surviving fabric reflects multiple phases, especially post-6th-century reconstruction.

name: Catacombs of Saint Callixtus (Rome)
description: Large Christian burial complex on the Appian Way, often used to illustrate funerary practice, martyr memory, and episcopal commemoration in Rome.
uncertainty: Dating and attribution of specific chambers and inscriptions can vary by excavation phase.

name: Insula Sacra / House of Peter (Capernaum)
description: Domestic-to-assembly structure in Capernaum, frequently cited in discussions of early house-church development and pilgrimage memory.
uncertainty: Identification with Peter's house is interpretive and not universally accepted as direct proof.

name: Etchmiadzin Cathedral
description: Armenian ecclesial center traditionally linked to the kingdom's early adoption of Christianity and to the see of the Catholicos.
uncertainty: Precise construction chronology and extent of earliest surviving masonry remain subjects of technical study.

name: Monastery of Saint Antony (Eastern Desert)
description: Anchoritic and cenobitic monastic landscape associated with Antony and formative Egyptian monastic traditions.
uncertainty: The current architectural footprint spans centuries beyond the earliest monastic occupation.

name: Basilica of Saint Simeon Stylites
description: Large pilgrimage complex around the pillar site of Simeon Stylites, reflecting the institutionalization of ascetic charisma.
uncertainty: The extant ruins document mature phases of pilgrimage architecture more than initial occupation.

name: Aquileia Basilica Complex
description: Northern Adriatic episcopal center with important mosaic floors and evidence for post-Constantinian Christian monumentalization.
uncertainty: Dating of some mosaic programs and liturgical spaces is refined continuously through archaeology.

name: Memorial Church of Moses (Mount Nebo)
description: Pilgrimage church and mosaic complex in the Transjordan commemorative landscape associated with Moses and late antique Christian travel.
uncertainty: Site interpretation relies on combining textual pilgrimage accounts with archaeological phases.
